Definition / Concept: The instrument shows an incorrect, fluctuating, or inconsistent measurement not matching the actual value. Often due to calibration drift or sensor fault.
Application Context: Instrumentation / Control systems – transmitters, sensors, gauges, PLC inputs.
Typical Examples: Pressure transmitter showing wrong reading; faulty thermocouple; false alarm.

Definition / Concept: Sudden, total, and catastrophic failure of a component or subassembly.
Typical Examples: Shaft breakage, bearing seizure, rotor destruction.

Definition / Concept: Hydraulic or electrical control line communicates unintentionally with the well fluids.
Typical Examples: Hydraulic crossflow between well and control line.

Definition / Concept: Failure to send, receive, or interpret a control or signal properly.
Typical Examples: Valve actuator not responding to command; PLC output lost.

Definition / Concept: Equipment performs its intended function, but with unacceptable delay.
Typical Examples: Slow valve actuation; delayed motor start.

Definition / Concept: External leakage of supplied fuel or gas.
Typical Examples: Diesel leakage at fuel pump or injector line.

Definition / Concept: Leakage of the main process fluid that the equipment is designed to contain, transport, or process. This includes production fluids such as crude oil, natural gas, process water, injection water, or any fluid that is part of the primary operational process. ELP applies to equipment with containment function (as per ISO 14224 tables B.6-B.13).
Application Context: Equipment with process containment function: Pumps (process fluid), Compressors (process gas), Valves (process lines), Heat exchangers (process side), Process piping, Pressure vessels, Separators.
Typical Examples: Pump mechanical seal leaking crude oil; process gas leak from compressor seal; gasket failure on process flange; injection water leak from valve body; product oil leak from heat exchanger tube.

Definition / Concept: Leakage of auxiliary/utility fluids that support the operation of equipment but are NOT part of the main process. Includes lubricating oil, cooling water, service air, hydraulic oil, seal flush fluids, and other support fluids. ELU does NOT apply to equipment without fluid systems (e.g., junction boxes, dry electrical enclosures). Valid only when equipment has utility fluid circuits (as per ISO 14224 tables B.6-B.13).
Application Context: Equipment with utility/auxiliary fluid systems: Lubrication systems, Cooling systems, Hydraulic systems, Pneumatic systems (service air), Seal support systems, Utility piping. NOT VALID for: Junction boxes, Terminal boxes, Dry electrical enclosures, Cable glands (no utility fluids present).
Typical Examples: Cooling-water hose rupture on heat exchanger; lube-oil leak from bearing housing; hydraulic oil leak from actuator; service air leak from instrument supply line; seal flush water leak. INVALID: Junction box (no fluids), Cable termination box (electrical only).

Definition / Concept: Output signal or performance is unstable, oscillating, or noisy.
Typical Examples: Regulator hunting; unstable transmitter signal.

Definition / Concept: Equipment fails to couple or connect as intended.
Typical Examples: Plug doesn’t engage; mechanical joint misaligned.

Definition / Concept: Cutting device or system fails to sever material.
Typical Examples: Shear ram fails to cut pipe.

Failure Mode for Failure Tree: FLP - Failure in lightning protection system
Definition / Concept: Lightning protection system not functioning properly.
Typical Examples: Ground resistance too high; open surge suppressor.

Definition / Concept: Output frequency outside the acceptable range.
Typical Examples: Generator frequency unstable or off nominal.

Definition / Concept: Output voltage out of design limits.
Typical Examples: Low generator voltage due to AVR fault.

Definition / Concept: Rotor or shaft does not rotate when power is applied.
Application Context: Rotating machinery – pumps, motors, compressors, turbines.
Typical Examples: Rotor seized; bearing locked.

Failure Mode for Failure Tree: FTC - Failure to close on demand
Definition / Concept: Component fails to close when commanded.
Typical Examples: ESD valve stuck open; breaker fails to trip.

Definition / Concept: Cannot isolate or disconnect when required.
Typical Examples: Breaker jammed open; valve won’t close.

Failure Mode for Failure Tree: FTF - Failure to function on demand
Definition / Concept: Complete functional failure when required.
Typical Examples: Emergency pump fails to start.

Failure Mode for Failure Tree: FTI - Failure to function as intended
Definition / Concept: Function achieved but outside specification or degraded.
Typical Examples: Control valve regulates incorrectly; low pump flow.

Definition / Concept: Locking mechanism fails to engage or release.
Typical Examples: Locking pin jammed; interlock not engaging.

Failure Mode for Failure Tree: FTO - Failure to open on demand
Definition / Concept: Equipment fails to open when commanded.
Typical Examples: Valve stem jammed; breaker contact stuck.

Definition / Concept: Inability to maintain controlled variable at setpoint.
Typical Examples: Pressure regulator oscillating or drifting.

Failure Mode for Failure Tree: FTS - Failure to start on demand
Definition / Concept: Equipment fails to start when commanded.
Typical Examples: Motor does not start; starter circuit fault.

Definition / Concept: Failure occurs during continuous operation.
Typical Examples: Pump stops due to bearing failure or overheating.

Definition / Concept: Output exceeds design or control limit.
Typical Examples: Over-speeding turbine; excessive flow or pressure.

Definition / Concept: Equipment fails to deliver heating.
Typical Examples: Heating element open circuit.

Definition / Concept: Thermal performance inadequate.
Typical Examples: Fouled exchanger tubes; low flow.

Definition / Concept: Process fluid leaks internally between internal zones.
Typical Examples: Internal seat leak in control valve.

Definition / Concept: Internal leak between utility circuits.
Typical Examples: Cooling water leaking between channels.

Definition / Concept: Internal leak with unknown fluid type.
Typical Examples: Leakage within component, fluid unidentified.

Definition / Concept: Lubrication oil pressure below minimum.
Typical Examples: Clogged filter; failed oil pump.

Definition / Concept: Leakage when the device is supposed to be closed.
Typical Examples: Valve seat wear causing bypass.

Definition / Concept: Sudden loss of mechanical or electrical load.
Typical Examples: Coupling breakage; generator load loss.

Definition / Concept: Loss of flotation or ballast integrity.
Typical Examples: Flooding of buoyancy tank.

Definition / Concept: Output lower than design value.
Typical Examples: Reduced flow or pressure.

Definition / Concept: Failure in mooring system integrity.
Typical Examples: Broken chain or shackle.

Definition / Concept: Abnormal or excessive noise.
Typical Examples: Bearing noise, cavitation.

Definition / Concept: No output despite running or energized condition.
Typical Examples: Pump running but no flow; zero signal output.

Definition / Concept: Temperature exceeds safe limit.
Typical Examples: Motor winding overheated; bearing temperature high.

Definition / Concept: Failure not covered by any specific code.
Typical Examples: Unclassified anomaly.

Definition / Concept: Component closes earlier than intended.
Typical Examples: Valve closes unexpectedly during operation.

Definition / Concept: Process variable deviates from allowable range.
Typical Examples: Pressure or temperature deviation.

Definition / Concept: Flow path blocked or obstructed.
Typical Examples: Blocked filter; fouled nozzle.

Description (ISO 14224): Loss of functions on both pods
Failure Mode for Failure Tree: POD - Loss of functions on both pods
Definition / Concept: Simultaneous loss of redundant functions.
Typical Examples: Both propulsion pods fail.

Definition / Concept: Insufficient energy to perform function.
Typical Examples: Voltage drop; low hydraulic pressure.

Definition / Concept: Loss of energy or signal between source and load.
Typical Examples: Broken cable; telemetry failure.

Definition / Concept: Build-up of sludge or deposits restricting function.
Typical Examples: Sludge accumulation in reservoir or filter.

Definition / Concept: Minor degradations without total failure.
Typical Examples: Small leak, slight vibration.

Definition / Concept: Inability to deploy or retrieve component.
Typical Examples: Tool fails to latch or release.

Definition / Concept: False high alarm signal.
Typical Examples: Level sensor falsely triggers high alarm.

Definition / Concept: False low alarm signal.
Typical Examples: Pressure transmitter drift causing false low alarm.

Definition / Concept: Relative motion between surfaces meant to remain fixed.
Typical Examples: Belt slip; coupling slippage.

Definition / Concept: Unintended activation or movement.
Typical Examples: Valve opens without command; relay self-triggering.

Definition / Concept: Loss of structural integrity or strength.
Typical Examples: Cracked housing; failed weld.

Failure Mode for Failure Tree: STP - Failure to stop on demand
Definition / Concept: Component fails to stop when commanded.
Typical Examples: Motor continues running after stop command.

Definition / Concept: Equipment immobile when motion is expected.
Typical Examples: Stem seized; shaft jammed.

Definition / Concept: Large-scale structural deformation under load.
Typical Examples: Pipe buckling due to thermal compression.

Definition / Concept: Failure occurred, but symptom not determined.
Typical Examples: Information insufficient for classification.

Definition / Concept: Uncommanded shutdown or stop.
Typical Examples: Motor tripped without cause.

Definition / Concept: Excessive or abnormal vibration.
Typical Examples: Unbalance, misalignment, looseness.

Definition / Concept: Output near zero but not total failure.
Typical Examples: Minimal flow; weak signal.

Definition / Concept: Unwanted communication between well and control line.
Typical Examples: Fluid backflow from well into hydraulic line.
